<!--
👋 Thanks for taking the time to contribute to my projects. I appreciate it!

Since I work solo on most of my projects, here's what helps me understand and review your changes:

1. What & why — What did you change and why? Even small fixes deserve a quick explanation.
2. Testing — How did you test this? Did you run it locally? Add new tests? Sometimes "tested manually" is perfectly fine.
3. Breaking changes — Does this change how existing code works? If so, let me know what might break and how to fix it.
4. Related issues — Link any issues this addresses (e.g., "Fixes #123" or "Closes #456").
5. Extra context — Anything else that helps: screenshots, deployment notes, tricky implementation details, etc.

💡 The more context you give me, the faster I can review and merge your contribution!

— William H.
-->
